notes: Pinned OCI SDK contract from the onboarding audit: CreateMacOrder, GetMacOrder, ListMacOrders, UpdateMacOrder, CancelMacOrder, ChangeMacOrderCompartment, and service-local GetWorkRequest helpers are present in github.com/oracle/oci-go-sdk/v65/mngdmac. The lifecycle enum is CREATING, UPDATING, ACTIVE, NEEDS_ATTENTION, DELETING, DELETED, and FAILED, while orderStatus carries customer-review, provisioning, and CANCELED phases.

Pinned OCI SDK contract from the onboarding audit: CreateMacOrderResponse returns a MacOrder body plus opc-work-request-id, while UpdateMacOrderResponse, CancelMacOrderResponse, and ChangeMacOrderCompartmentResponse return work-request headers only.

Pinned provider coverage is unconfirmed. formal/sources.lock still points at github.com/oracle/terraform-provider-oci@eb653febb1bab4cc6650a96d404a8baf36fdf671, but the accessible provider inventory did not expose a matching MacOrder resource or data source. The providerResource and datasource names therefore stay provisional OCI-style placeholders so generator-owned registry artifacts can derive stable links without claiming verified provider coverage.

Repo-authored runtime contract stays narrower than the imported SDK surface: only displayName, ipRange, orderDescription, orderSize, and shape reconcile in place. ChangeMacOrderCompartment remains out of scope, so compartmentId and commitmentTerm stay replacement-only drift; optional empty strings do not force blind clears for displayName or ipRange.

Repo-authored bind-versus-create stays explicit: pre-create lookup lists by exact compartmentId plus displayName, reuses only a unique candidate in ACTIVE, CREATING, or UPDATING, and fails duplicate exact-name matches instead of guessing.

Kubernetes delete maps to CancelMacOrder rather than a nonexistent DeleteMacOrder API. The reviewed runtime follows the cancel work request through GetWorkRequest, then rereads GetMacOrder or ListMacOrders until OCI reports DELETED, NotFound, or orderStatus CANCELED before releasing the finalizer.
